channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Two Calgary paramedics injured in non-life-threatening ambulance accident
item.description: Two paramedics in Calgary were involved in an ambulance crash, resulting in injuries that are reported as non-life-threatening. Emergency services responded quickly to the incident, ensuring the param...

item.title: US and Iran Declare Ceasefire Ahead of Islamabad Negotiations
item.description: A ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been declared effective immediately, signalling a pause after weeks of escalating tensions. Pakistan's Prime Minister, Shehbaz Sharif, confirmed the ...

item.title: Trump announces two-week ceasefire with Iran after submitting peace proposal
item.description: Stock markets surged following a dramatic announcement from US President Donald Trump, who declared a two-week ceasefire with Iran after intense negotiations. The ceasefire, brokered by Pakistan and s...

item.title: Chelsea Blackwell and Jimmy Presnell split before their wedding on Love Is Blind
item.description: Chelsea Blackwell, the American reality TV star known from season six of Love Is Blind, didn’t make it to the altar with her fiancé Jimmy Presnell. The couple initially got engaged during the show, fi...

item.title: Artemis II crew begins return journey to Earth after lunar mission
item.description: The Artemis II mission crew, comprising NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Canadian Space Agency astronaut Jeremy Hansen, has commenced the return leg of their journey fo...

item.title: Europe must strengthen its own resilience amid ongoing US political instability
item.description: Rafael Behr argues that Europe cannot assume the United States will return to stable democratic norms after Donald Trump's presidency. Despite Trump's authoritarian tendencies, the US remains a democr...

item.title: Government Faces Pressure to Curb Spending Amid Rising Fuel Prices and Subsidy Proposal
item.description: Tanzania's government is under increasing pressure to protect its economy from the impact of rising global fuel prices. Economic analysts are advising strict spending control, while the ruling party (...

item.title: Kelly Khumalo to perform at Soulful Sundays in KuGompo City, joined by local acts
item.description: Singer Kelly Khumalo is set to headline Soulful Sundays at the Eastern Cape Jazz Festival, scheduled for April 26 at Ronnie’s Motors in Beacon Bay, KuGompo City. The event, which began in 2022, will f...

item.title: Bukoba Development Projects Progressing Well Under TACTIC Initiative
item.description: Bukoba Urban Member of Parliament, Engineer Johnstone Mutasingwa, has assured residents that major development projects in the municipality are moving according to plan and will be finished on time to...

item.title: Kanayo O. Kanayo pushes for lifetime streaming royalties for Nollywood actors
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actor Kanayo O. Kanayo has called on the Actors Guild of Nigeria (AGN) to introduce a policy that will give actors lifetime streaming royalties whenever their movies are shown on pla...

item.title: Tensions Escalate Between EC and TMC Ahead of Bengal Elections
item.description: The Election Commission of India (ECI) has assured that the upcoming 2026 West Bengal Assembly elections will be conducted in a 'fear-free, violence-free, and intimidation-free' environment. This stat...

item.title: Unseasonably warm temperatures to end as cooler weather sets in
item.description: Wednesday is expected to feel more like summer than early April, with warm, sunny weather continuing across much of the UK. The previous day, the UK saw temperatures reach 25°C (77°F) in Mona, Anglese...

item.title: No Nationwide Ban on Cellphone Use; Viral Video Proven AI-Generated
item.description: A recent viral video claiming that the Philippine government will implement a nationwide ban on cellphone use starting April 15, 2026, has been proven false. The video, posted on March 30, garnered al...

item.title: Tanzania Commission of Inquiry into October 2025 Incidents Gets Additional 21 Days Extension
item.description: The Independent Commission of Inquiry investigating the incidents that occurred on 29th October 2025 in Tanzania has received an additional 21 days to finish its important work. The new deadline is no...

item.title: Survey shows Filipinos focus on economic concerns during Middle East tensions
item.description: A recent WR Numero survey reported by ABS-CBN News reveals that Filipinos are giving higher importance to economic relief and practical needs instead of politics, as global tensions in the Middle East...

item.title: Pakistan Stock Exchange sees record rally after US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) witnessed an extraordinary surge on Wednesday as the KSE-100 index jumped by 12,362.38 points, or 8.15 percent, reaching 164,035.83 by mid-morning. This sharp rally c...

item.title: Congress Criticises PM Modi's Foreign Policy Amid Iran-US Ceasefire
item.description: The Congress party has sharply criticised Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s foreign policy, focusing on the timing of his Israel visit which occurred just two days before the Israel-US military action ag...

item.title: Dr Zarak Bhat from NIT Srinagar joins AI research hub at University of Edinburgh
item.description: Dr Zarak Bhat, an accomplished scholar from the National Institute of Technology (NIT) Srinagar, has been inducted into the AI hub at the University of Edinburgh, marking a significant milestone in hi...

item.title: London to experience warmer weather than Barcelona with 25C temperatures this Wednesday
item.description: Temperatures in London are forecast to soar to 25C on Wednesday, making the capital hotter than Barcelona, which will only reach 19C. According to the Met Office, the warm weather will extend across m...

item.title: Nearly 400 Gas Stations Closed Across the Philippines Amidst Fuel Price Hikes
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has reported a significant increase in the number of gas stations temporarily closed due to the ongoing challenges posed by rising fuel prices. As of April 8, 2026...
